title: Find Log Gaps
description: Given a log file with one Unix timestamp per line, find periods where no log events were recorded for longer than a specified gap duration. Timestamps may be out of order and should be sorted first. Return a list of (gap_start, gap_end, duration_seconds) tuples for all gaps that exceed the threshold.
constraints: Timestamps are Unix integers, one per line, possibly out of order
Only report gaps strictly greater than gap_seconds
Return results as (gap_start, gap_end, duration) sorted by gap_start ascending

examples.explanation: Between timestamps 1010 and 1080 there is a 70-second gap which exceeds the 30-second threshold. All other consecutive pairs have gaps of 5 seconds.

starter_code.python:
def find_gaps(log_content: str, gap_seconds: int = 30) -> list[tuple[int, int, int]]:
    pass

test_cases.description: One gap of 70 seconds exceeds the 30s threshold
All gaps of 10 seconds, none exceed 30s threshold
Out-of-order timestamps with two large gaps after sorting
